tagline: Every apple experience under one roof.
elevator_pitch: The Orchard is an immersive apple-themed destination where families, couples, and friends come to pick apples, press fresh cider, bake pies, and dive into the world's deepest leaf pit. Think of it as the ultimate fall experience hub that turns a simple fruit into an all-day adventure.
problem: Fall experiences like corn mazes and pumpkin patches have become stale and repetitive. Families crave fresh, interactive seasonal activities but keep recycling the same tired options year after year. There is no single destination that brings together the full spectrum of apple and autumn experiences in one memorable place.
solution: The Orchard is a large-format experiential venue built around a single theme: apples. Guests can pick from dozens of apple varieties, press their own cider, bake pies in guided workshops, and experience viral attractions like the world's deepest leaf pit with slow-motion cameras. It combines education, food, entertainment, and social media moments into one destination.
target_market: Families with children ages 3 to 14 looking for weekend activities, couples seeking unique date nights, friend groups wanting Instagram-worthy seasonal outings, and schools or corporate teams booking group experiences. Primary geography: suburban and semi-rural areas within 60 minutes of major metro regions.
revenue_model: Tiered admission tickets ($15 to $45 per person depending on experience level), add-on workshops (pie baking, cider pressing at $20 to $35 each), retail sales of fresh apples, cider, pies, and branded merchandise, private event bookings for birthdays and corporate outings, and seasonal pop-up cider stands at partner locations like Spirit Halloween stores.
key_features: Pick-your-own orchard with 20+ apple varieties and guided tastings
Hands-on cider pressing and pie baking workshops for all ages
The Leaf Pit: a viral-ready, deep-dive leaf pool with slow-motion cameras and diving boards
Seasonal rotating experiences including harvest festivals, night markets, and holiday events
competitive_advantage: Unlike generic pumpkin patches and corn mazes, The Orchard builds an entire brand ecosystem around a single beloved ingredient. The combination of experiential workshops, viral attractions like the Leaf Pit, and year-round retail creates multiple revenue streams and repeat visitation that typical seasonal venues cannot match.
market_size_estimate: The U.S. agritourism market generates over $950 million annually, and the broader experiential entertainment market exceeds $12 billion. Apple orchards alone attract over 100 million visitors per year. A single Orchard location in a strong metro-adjacent market could realistically capture $2 to $4 million in annual revenue.
go_to_market: Launch a flagship location near a major metro area in a region with strong fall culture (Northeast or Mountain West). Build pre-launch buzz through Instagram and TikTok content featuring the Leaf Pit. Partner with local schools for field trips and offer early-bird season passes to build a loyal base before opening weekend.
risks: Heavy seasonality: peak revenue concentrated in September through November, requiring creative off-season programming
High upfront capital for land, structures, and orchard establishment with a multi-year timeline to mature apple trees
Weather dependency: rain, early frost, or drought can significantly impact both crop yield and visitor turnout
year_one_milestones: Secure a 20+ acre site and complete venue build-out including workshop spaces, retail barn, and Leaf Pit
Attract 50,000 visitors in the first fall season through social media virality and local press coverage
Generate $1.5 million in combined revenue from admissions, workshops, retail, and events
